form_answers.question: Are community classes currently approved in the budget?
Does the class provide opportunity for community inclusion?
Does the class accommodate the individual’s health and/or safety needs?
Does the class increase independence or substitute for human assistance?
Is the class provided exclusively for the benefit of the participant?
Is the class open to and attended by the broader public?
Does the class have published fees? (attach link / advertised flyer)
Are the fees identical for both OPWDD and non-OPWDD individuals?
Is the class subject based? (Art, Dance, Martial Arts, Cooking)
Does the class provide college credits?
Is the class clinical in nature? (therapy)
Is the class given in a setting accessed only by people with Developmental Disabilities?
Is the class run by OPWDD or a provider-agency staff acting in their official capacities?
Is the class located on grounds where OPWDD services are normally provided?
Does the class duplicate any Medicaid state plan or HCBS waiver services (or entity that delivers such)?
Does the class duplicate services given through Board of Education for school-aged children?
Is there a published schedule of the class? (please attach)
Is the class being reimbursed directly? (if yes, attach a W9)

findings.criterion_text: Class is open to and attended by the broader public (not restricted to disabled/OPWDD individuals)
findings.note: Could not find clear language about public access — please check manually.

findings.criterion_text: Class has published fees (a public price is visible)
findings.note: No price was visible on the page (sites often block automated readers) — please confirm the published fee manually.

findings.criterion_text: Fees are identical for OPWDD and non-OPWDD individuals (no special/segregated pricing)
findings.note: No price was found, so identical-pricing could not be confirmed — please review.

findings.criterion_text: Class is subject-based (art, dance, martial arts, cooking, etc.)
findings.note: The page describes a subject-based activity ('art').

findings.criterion_text: Class does NOT give college credits
findings.note: No mention of college credit found on the page (inferred from absence).

findings.criterion_text: Class is NOT clinical/therapy in nature
findings.note: No clinical/therapy framing found on the page (inferred from absence).

findings.criterion_text: Fee-match: published fee is consistent with the 'Fee per Session' on the form
findings.note: Could not parse a fee from both the form and the website — please compare manually.

findings.criterion_text: Community classes are currently approved in the participant's budget
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.criterion_text: Class provides opportunity for community inclusion
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.criterion_text: Class increases independence or substitutes for human assistance
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.criterion_text: Class does not duplicate Medicaid state plan / HCBS waiver / Board of Education services

findings.criterion_text: Class relates to a habilitative need in the Life Plan (valued outcome)

summary: Automated rule-based check of Gracie Barra's website found 4 of 8 website-verifiable items, with 4 needing manual review. No AI model was used for this review — a human should double-check any 'Needs Review' items before deciding.
